concerns.text: T006.3 mandates raising a 'critical error' if a real dataset fetch fails, directly violating spec.md Edge Cases and FR-001 which require the system to 'skip that image and log the error rather than crashing'. This task weakens the robustness constraint of the spec.

T018 requires raising a 'critical error' and aborting the pipeline if <30 images are processed. The spec Edge Cases state the system 'MUST skip that image... and continue' without mandating a hard pipeline failure for the entire batch if the threshold isn't met. This task silently relaxes the 'continue processing' requirement into a hard stop.

T006.1 defines the baseline stimulus generation as 'Gaussian noise and random geometric shapes'. This contradicts FR-001 and spec.md US-1 which require 'baseline images from Visual Genome' (or a verified substitute like COCO per plan.md). Generating synthetic noise images instead of real scene images fundamentally violates the data source constraint of the FR.

T026 implements a 2-minute distractor task but omits the explicit tolerance check logic (±10s) required by spec.md US-2 Acceptance Scenario 2. Without this verification step in the task, the acceptance criterion for timing precision is dropped.

T027 relies on a 'set difference' to generate false details but does not include a verification step to ensure the resulting pool contains at least 10 unique items (as required by US-2 Scenario 3). If the baseline image contains many mock objects, the task may fail to generate the required number of lure questions, dropping the acceptance criterion.
